key_results: Sequential dose reductions to 5 mg daily and 5 mg every other day, as well as dose delays, were allowed for toxicity [see Dosage and Administration ( 2.2 )] .
Transfusion independence was seen in 99/148 (67%) patients (95% CI [59, 74]).
A second dose reduction or interruption due to adverse events was required in 50 (33.8%) of the 148 patients.

key_results: Of the total population of 31 patients treated for MDS/MPD, 14 (45%) achieved a complete hematological response and 12 (39%) a major cytogenetic response (including 10 with a CCyR).

A second induction was highly recommended for patients who did not achieve a response and was mandatory for patients achieving greater than 50% reduction in percent blasts.

key_results: Of the total population of 31 patients treated for MDS/MPD, 14 (45%) achieved a complete hematological response and 12 (39%) a major cytogenetic response (including 10 with a CCyR).
Only 1 (7%) out of the 14 patients without a translocation associated with PDGFR gene re-arrangement achieved a complete hematological response and none achieved a major cytogenetic response.

Median duration of therapy was 12.9 months (0.8 to 26.7) in the 7 patients treated within the Phase 2 study and ranged between 1 week and more than 18 months in responding patients in the published literature.
